channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump's pick for hostage negotiations envoy withdraws nomination but will continue on hostage issues
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Adam Boehler, President Donald Trump’s choice to become special envoy for hostage affairs, has withdrawn his nomination in order to avoid a requirement that he divest from his investment business.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US prepares to deport about 300 alleged gang members to El Salvador
item.description: El Salvador is agreeing to accept from the United States about 300 alleged members of a Venezuelan gang for one year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: House Democrats to hold 'Day of Action' to push back against GOP-backed spending bill
item.description: House Democrats will hold a "Day of Action" across the country on Tuesday to push back against the GOP-backed spending bill to avert a government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats confront limits of their minority power after bruising shutdown vote
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senate Democrats were grim Friday as they left Washington after a brutal 10-week stretch that consistently showed the limits of their power in the minority — and culminated with a deeply personal rupture over how to best counter President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic Party fractures in government shutdown fight, with anger running high
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Democratic Party was fracturing Friday as a torrent of frustration and anger was unleashed at Senate Democrats, led by Sen.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, MATT BROWN and STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House lawmakers hold hearing to further investigate Florida insurance companies
item.description: House lawmakers gathered at the statehouse Friday for a hearing to look into a bombshell report that Florida insurance companies were "ripping off" Floridians and misleading the legislature, claiming to be broke.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump undoes stack of 'harmful' Biden executive orders, shrinks multiple agencies
item.description: The White House on Friday said President Donald Trump took more actions to downsize the federal government through several executive orders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Appeals court lifts blocks on Trump's orders restricting diversity, equity and inclusion programs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An appeals court on Friday lifted a block on executive orders seeking to end government support for diversity, equity and inclusion programs, handing the Trump administration a win after a string of setbacks defending President Donald Trump’s agenda from dozens of lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate approves funding bill hours before shutdown deadline, sending to Trump for signature
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate passed a Republican-led spending bill Friday hours before a government shutdown, overcoming sharp Democratic opposition to the measure and sending it to President Donald Trump to be signed into law.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio says South Africa’s ambassador to the US 'is no longer welcome' in the country
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Friday that South Africa’s ambassador to the United States “is no longer welcome” in the country, in the latest Trump administration move targeting the African nation.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Politics Friday: Minnesota’s paid leave law remains a flashpoint as launch nears
item.description: MPR News senior politics editor Brian Bakst talks with two Minnesota lawmakers about the state’s new paid leave program — scheduled to start in January of next year — and the legislation that could possibly delay or change eligibility.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: AOC, Sanders tell supporters to mask up for West Coast town halls
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders, I-Vt., and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, D-N.Y., are taking the "Fighting Oligarchy" rallies out West next week – and advising supporters to mask up to participate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minnesota National Guard members receive email instructing those with gender dysphoria to voluntarily separate
item.description: The email to National Guard members lays out a timeline for service members who experience gender dysphoria to voluntarily separate from the military.
item.dc:creator: Erica Zurek and The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Nevada senators split on vote ahead of potential government shutdown
item.description: Nevada's two Democratic senators split their decisions Friday ahead of a vote in the U.S. Senate to avoid a potential partial federal government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DHS agents search Columbia dorm rooms after Khalil arrest
item.description: Department of Homeland Security agents searched two dorm rooms at Columbia University Thursday night, the school said, a move that comes less than a week after the federal arrest of former grad student Mahmoud Khalil.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE lease cancellations: These government offices could be closed in Minnesota
item.description: The General Services Administration, working with Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency, has notified landlords that federal agencies will be terminating hundreds of leases for offices and buildings in the coming months — including a dozen in Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Death penalty, guns topics of 2 bills introduced in Nevada Senate
item.description: If it seems like death penalty cases drag out in the courts already, new legislation introduced on Thursday would ensure that the process slows down even more.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: U.S. inflation cooled last month, though trade war threatens to lift prices
item.description: U.S. inflation slowed last month for the first time since September even as additional tariffs on steel and aluminum kicked in Wednesday that could send prices higher.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Florida seeks easier voting access for hurricane-displaced citizens
item.description: Last year's hurricanes displaced thousands of Floridians, turning their lives upside down and making it even harder for some to vote during a crucial election year. Now, a Pinellas County state lawmaker wants to make it easier for people to cast their ballots after a storm.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Race for NYC mayor: Public safety and Cuomo defense money
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Candidates in the race for New York City mayor continue to take aim at former Gov. Andrew Cuomo's public safety agenda and tax dollars being spent on his legal troubles.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: President Trump's tariffs raise concerns of economic recession
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – President Donald Trump’s tariffs on products from Canada and Mexico have some Americans worried that they will lead the economy into a recession.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11
